what_it_does: Renders a two-phone app-flow mockup where the first screen combines a warm workshop hero with work rows, stats, action, and navigation icons, while the second screen shows a close confirmation state.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans, IBM Plex Mono, shared piece and phone styles, and a shared organisms runtime.
Defines file-local CSS for the fused hero band and role labels.
Shows two phone mockups connected by a joint element.
First phone includes a warm radial hero, stats band, generated ledger rows, a call-to-action, and runtime-generated rail icons.
Second phone centers a gold checkmark, the word Kept, a short explanatory line, and an ochre hatch.
standouts: The first screen explicitly labels itself open + work · fused.
The visual composition places a warm hero band above cooler work content in one phone frame.
Rows and rail icons are populated by inline JavaScript rather than static markup.
conventions: Uses shared CSS custom properties for type, spacing, and color.
Uses existing phone/piece class names for the outer composition.
Uses small inline data arrays to generate repeated UI content.
Mixes shared classes with inline style attributes for local layout and positioning.
concerns: Inline style attributes fix several layout positions and dimensions directly in the markup.
The script assumes DNA.org.icon and the rows and rail elements are available, with no local fallback if the runtime fails.
notes: A compact two-screen phone composition showing a fused open/work screen followed by a close confirmation screen.
questions: Should the rolecap positions remain hard-coded for this mockup size?
Are the generated rail icons meant to be interactive or only visual?
Is the phrase Enter Today's Cut intended as a static label or a functioning action?
What should happen if the shared organisms runtime is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the rendered content: the file shows a short app flow where open and work are fused on the first screen and close remains a separate complete state.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make a design principle visible by showing how open, work, and close roles can appear across a short two-screen mobile flow.
novelty: The distinct move is the explicit fusion of open and work inside one phone screen, with a labeled role marker and a connector to a separate close state.
contribution: Contributes a file-local pattern for representing multiple design roles in a single mobile flow: fused open/work content with ledger rows and navigation, paired with a complete close confirmation state.
summary_for_embedding: This is a standalone HTML mobile mockup rendering a two-screen app flow with a fused workshop-and-ledger screen followed by a kept confirmation screen.
intention_for_embedding: This was for visualizing how a short app flow can fuse open and work roles while preserving a distinct close moment.
